rules: Shuffle your deck!

attacks.name: Torrent Blade
attacks.effect: Return 2 Water Energy attached to Suicune & Entei LEGEND to your hand. Choose 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. This attack does 100 damage to that Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness or Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Bursting Inferno
attacks.effect: The Defending Pokémon is now Burned.

rules:
Put this card from your hand onto your Bench only with the other half of Suicune & Entei LEGEND.

When this Pokémon has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Torrent Blade
attacks.effect: Return 2 Water Energy attached to Suicune & Entei LEGEND to your hand. Choose 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. This attack does 100 damage to that Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness or Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Bursting Inferno
attacks.effect: The Defending Pokémon is now Burned.

rules:
Put this card from your hand onto your Bench only with the other half of Suicune & Entei LEGEND.

When this Pokémon has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Thunderbolt Spear
attacks.effect: Raikou & Suicune LEGEND does 50 damage to itself and don't apply Weakness to this damage.

attacks.name: Aurora Gain
attacks.effect: Remove 5 damage counters from Raikou & Suicune LEGEND.

rules:
Put this card from your hand onto your Bench only with the other half of Raikou & Suicune LEGEND.

When this Pokémon has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Thunderbolt Spear
attacks.effect: Raikou & Suicune LEGEND does 50 damage to itself and don't apply Weakness to this damage.

attacks.name: Aurora Gain
attacks.effect: Remove 5 damage counters from Raikou & Suicune LEGEND.

rules:
Put this card from your hand onto your Bench only with the other half of Raikou & Suicune LEGEND.

When this Pokémon has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Detonation Spin
attacks.effect: Discard a Fire Energy attached to Entei & Raikou LEGEND.

attacks.name: Thunder Fall
attacks.effect: Discard all Energy attached to Entei & Raikou LEGEND. This attack does 80 damage to each Pokémon that has any Poké-Powers (both yours and your opponent's). This attack's damage isn't affected by Weakness or Resistance.

rules:
Put this card from your hand onto your Bench only with the other half of Entei & Raikou LEGEND.

When this Pokémon has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Detonation Spin
attacks.effect: Discard a Fire Energy attached to Entei & Raikou LEGEND.

attacks.name: Thunder Fall
attacks.effect: Discard all Energy attached to Entei & Raikou LEGEND. This attack does 80 damage to each Pokémon that has any Poké-Powers (both yours and your opponent's). This attack's damage isn't affected by Weakness or Resistance.

rules:
Put this card from your hand onto your Bench only with the other half of Entei & Raikou LEGEND.

When this Pokémon has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Hammer Arm
attacks.effect: Discard the top card from your opponent's deck.

attacks.name: Darkness Howl
attacks.effect: This attack does 20 damage to each Pokémon in play (both yours and your opponent's) (excluding any Darkness Pokémon). (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Power Claw
attacks.effect: This attack's damage isn't affected by Poké-Powers, Poké-Bodies, or any other effects on the Defending Pokémon.

attacks.name: Megaton Tail
attacks.effect: Discard the top 3 cards of your deck.

attacks.name: Energy Stream
attacks.effect: Search your discard pile for an Energy card and attach it to Steelix.

attacks.name: Gaia Crush
attacks.effect: You may discard any Stadium card in play.

attacks.name: Powerful Spark
attacks.effect: Does 40 damage plus 10 more damage for each Energy attached to all of your Pokémon.

attacks.name: Dragon Steam
attacks.effect: If your opponent has any Fire Pokémon in play, this attack's base damage is 20 instead of 60.

attacks.name: Severe Poison
attacks.effect: The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned. Put 4 damage counters instead of 1 on that Pokémon between turns.

attacks.name: Skill Dive
attacks.effect: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. This attack does 30 damage to that Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

rules: Flip a coin. If heads, return 1 of your Pokémon and all cards attached to it to your hand.

Choose 1 of your Basic Pokémon in play. If you have a Stage 1 or Stage 2 card that evolves from that Pokémon in your hand, put that card on the Basic Pokémon. (This counts as evolving that Pokémon.) If you choose a Stage 2 Pokémon in your hand, put that Pokémon on the Basic Pokémon instead of on a Stage 1 Pokémon.
Your opponent switches his or her Active Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.

Attach PlusPower to 1 of your Pokémon. Discard this card at the end of your turn.

If the Pokémon PlusPower is attached to attacks, the attack does 10 more damage to the Defending Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flip a coin. If heads, choose 1 of your Pokémon, and remove all Special Conditions and 6 damage counters from that Pokémon (all if there are less than 6).

You can play only one Supporter card each turn. When you play this card, put it next to your Active Pokémon. When your turn ends, discard this card.

Each player shuffles his or her hand into his or her deck and draws 4 cards.

You can play only one Supporter card each turn. When you play this card, put it next to your Active Pokémon. When your turn ends, discard this card.

Look at the top 8 cards of your deck. Choose as many Energy cards as you like, show them to your opponent, and put them into your hand. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck.

Flip a coin. If heads, search your discard pile for a Pokémon, show it to your opponent, and put it on top of your deck. If tails, search your discard pile for a Trainer card, show it to your opponent, and put it on top of your deck.

You can play only one Supporter card each turn. When you play this card, put it next to your Active Pokémon. When your turn ends, discard this card.

Discard an Energy card from your hand. Then, draw 4 cards.
Search your discard pile for 4 basic Energy cards, show them to your opponent, and shuffle them into your deck.

You can play only one Supporter card each turn. When you play this card, put it next to your Active Pokémon. When your turn ends, discard this card.

Flip a coin. If heads, draw 3 cards. If tails, draw 2 cards.
Flip 2 coins. For each heads, search your deck for a Basic Pokémon, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. If you do, shuffle your deck afterward.

You can play only one Supporter card each turn. When you play this card, put it next to your Active Pokémon. When your turn ends, discard this card.

Draw 3 cards. Your opponent may draw a card.

attacks.name: Double Attack
attacks.effect: Choose 2 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. This attack does 10 damage to each of them. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: Capable of flying safely in dark places, it emits ultrasonic cries to check for any obstacles.

attacks.name: Speed Evolution
attacks.effect: Search your deck for a card that evolves from Weedle and put it onto Weedle. (This counts as evolving Weedle.) Shuffle your deck afterward.

flavorText: Its poison stinger is very powerful. Its bright-colored body is intended to warn off its enemies.

attacks.name: Fireworks
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If tails, discard a Fire Energy attached to Vulpix.

flavorText: As it develops, its single white tail gains color and splits into six. It is quite warm and cuddly.

attacks.name: Leech Seed
attacks.effect: If this attack does any damage to the Defending Pokémon (after applying Weakness and Resistance), remove 1 damage counter from Turtwig.

flavorText: Photosynthesis occurs across its body under the sun. The shell on its back is actually hardened soil.

attacks.name: Green Call
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. For each heads, search your deck for a Grass Pokémon, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. If you do, shuffle your deck afterward.

flavorText: The bunch of fruit around its neck ripens twice a year and is delicious. It's a highly favored tropical snack.

attacks.name: Take Down
attacks.effect: Teddiursa does 10 damage to itself.

flavorText: Before food becomes scarce in wintertime, its habit is to hoard food in many hidden locations.

attacks.name: Call for Family
attacks.effect: Search your deck for up to 2 Basic Pokémon and put them onto your Bench. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Mystifying Horns
attacks.effect: The Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: The curved antlers subtly change the flow of air to create a strange space where reality is distorted.
The shell is soft when it is born. It soon becomes so resilient, prodding fingers will bounce off it.

attacks.name: Roost
attacks.effect: Remove 4 damage counters from Spearow. Spearow can't retreat during your next turn.

flavorText: It flaps its short wings to flush out insects from tall grass. It then plucks them with its stubby beak.

attacks.name: Petal Dance
attacks.effect: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads. Roselia is now Confused.

flavorText: Roselia that drink nutritionally rich springwater are said to reveal rare coloration when they bloom.

attacks.name: Double Chop
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: They communicate with one another using their auras. They are able to run all through the night.

attacks.name: Water Arrow
attacks.effect: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. This attack does 20 damage to that Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: It has superb accuracy. The water it shoots out can strike even moving prey from more than 300 feet.

attacks.name: Call for Family
attacks.effect: Search your deck for a Basic Pokémon and put it onto your Bench. Shuffle your deck afterward.

flavorText: The direction of its belly spiral differs by area. The equator is thought to have an effect on this.

attacks.name: Swing Around
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 damage plus 20 more damage for each heads.

flavorText: It twists and squirms through the ground. The thunderous roar of its tunneling echoes a long way.

attacks.name: Boundless Power
attacks.effect: Onix can't attack during your next turn.

flavorText: It rapidly bores through the ground at 50 mph by squirming and twisting its massive, rugged body.

attacks.name: Teleport
attacks.effect: Switch Natu with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: It usually forages for food on the ground but may, on rare occasions, hop onto branches to peck at shoots.

attacks.name: Sharpshooting
attacks.effect: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. This attack does 10 damage to that Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: It likes playing mischievous tricks such as screaming and wailing to startle people at night.

attacks.name: Karate Chop
attacks.effect: Does 40 damage minus 10 damage for each damage counter on Mankey.

flavorText: It is extremely ill-tempered. Groups of them will attack any handy target for no reason.
It dislikes cold places, so it blows scorching flames to make the environment suitable for itself.
It is born deep underground. It can't emerge until it has entirely consumed the soil around it.

attacks.name: Mountain Eater
attacks.effect: Discard the top card of your opponent's deck. Then, remove 2 damage counters from Larvitar.

attacks.name: Reckless Charge
attacks.effect: Larvitar does 10 damage to itself.

flavorText: It feeds on soil. After it has eaten a large mountain, it will fall asleep so it can grow.

attacks.name: Fin Smack
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: Its big, developed fins move rapidly, allowing it to swim backward while still facing forward.
It shoots positive and negative electricity between the tips of its two antennae and zaps its enemies.
It evolves by sucking the energy out of the small ball where it had been storing nutrients.

attacks.name: Drawup Power
attacks.effect: Search your deck for an Energy card, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

flavorText: It walks around on its tentacles in search of a tree branch where it can dangle down and ambush prey.

attacks.name: Muddy Water
attacks.effect: Does 10 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: It inflates its flotation sac, keeping its face above water in order to watch for prey movement.

attacks.name: Reaction
attacks.effect: You may switch Beldum with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: The magnetic force generated by its body repels the ground's natural magnetism, letting it float.

attacks.name: Tail Code
attacks.effect: Move an Energy card attached to the Defending Pokémon to another of your opponent's Pokémon.

attacks.name: Tail Smash
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: It lives atop tall trees. When leaping from branch to branch, it deftly uses its tail for balance.

attacks.name: Water Arrow
attacks.effect: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. This attack does 20 damage to that Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: It is a well-established symbol of longevity. If its shell has algae on it, that Wartortle is very old.

attacks.name: Crimson Bull
attacks.effect: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads. Tauros is now Confused.

flavorText: They fight each other by locking horns. The herd's protector takes pride in its battle-scarred horns.

attacks.name: Muddy Water
attacks.effect: Does 10 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: Its fin tips leak poison. Its fins and bones are highly valued as ingredients in herbal medicine.
Even sealed in its shell, it can move freely. Hard and fast, it has outstanding destructive power.

attacks.name: Rage
attacks.effect: Does 20 damage plus 10 more damage for each damage counter on Pupitar.

flavorText: Its shell is as hard as sheet rock, and it is also very strong. Its thrashing can topple a mountain.

attacks.name: Hypnoblast
attacks.effect: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: The skin on most of its body is moist. However, the skin on its belly spiral feels smooth.

attacks.name: Collect
attacks.effect: Draw 2 cards.

attacks.name: Thunder Jolt
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If tails, Plusle does 10 damage to itself.

flavorText: It absorbs electricity from telephone poles. It shorts out its body to create crackling noises.

attacks.name: Flare Bonus
attacks.effect: Discard a Fire Energy card from your hand. Then, draw 3 cards.

flavorText: The flaming magma it stores in the hump on its back is the source of its tremendous power.

attacks.name: Call for Family
attacks.effect: Search your deck for up to 2 Basic Pokémon and put them onto your Bench. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Tag Team Boost
attacks.effect: If Plusle is on your Bench, this attack does 10 damage plus 20 more damage.

flavorText: Exposure to electricity from Minun and Plusle promotes blood circulation and relaxes muscles.

attacks.name: Energy Crane
attacks.effect: Search your discard pile for up to 2 Psychic Energy cards and attach them to your Pokémon in any way you like.

flavorText: When two Beldum fuse together, a magnetic nervous system places their brains in union.

attacks.name: Speed Evolution
attacks.effect: Search your deck for a card that evolves from Kakuna and put it onto Kakuna. (This counts as evolving Kakuna.) Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Poison Sting
attacks.effect: The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: Although it is a cocoon, it can move a little. It can extend its poison barb if it is attacked.

attacks.name: Sleep Powder
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: A Grotle that lives in the forest is said to have its own secret springwater.

attacks.name: Mean Look
attacks.effect: The Defending Pokémon can't retreat during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: It can drink more than 10 ounces of blood at once. If it has too much, it gets heavy and flies clumsily.

attacks.name: Return
attacks.effect: Draw cards until you have 6 cards in your hand.

flavorText: When spotted, this Pokémon escapes backward by furiously boring into the ground with its tail.
During times of strong sunlight, its bud blooms, its petals open fully, and it becomes very active.

attacks.name: Cross Chop
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 50 damage plus 30 more damage.

flavorText: With its ability to distinguish any aroma, it unfailing finds all food buried deep underground.

attacks.name: Tail Crush
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 40 damage plus 20 more damage.

attacks.name: Hyper Beam
attacks.effect: Discard an Energy card attached to the Defending Pokémon.

flavorText: Extremely strong, it can change the landscape. It has an insolent nature that makes it not care about others.
You find abandoned coal mines full of them. They dig tirelessly in search of coal.

attacks.name: Guard Press
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, any damage done to Steelix by attacks is reduced by 20 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Steel Swing
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 80 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: Its body has been compressed deep under the ground. As a result, it is even harder than a diamond.

attacks.name: Power Blow
attacks.effect: Does 20 damage times the amount of Energy attached to Roserade.

flavorText: Its sweet aroma attracts prey. Then it spews poison. The more toxic it is, the sweeter its aroma.

attacks.name: Bebop Punch
attacks.effect: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 50 damage times the number of heads to that Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: If approached while asleep, it may awaken and angrily give chase in a groggy state of semi-sleep.

attacks.name: Steamroll
attacks.effect: Does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Dynamic Punch
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 60 damage plus 40 more damage and the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: Although an energetic, skilled swimmer that uses all of its muscles, it lives on dry land.

attacks.name: Heat Acceleration
attacks.effect: Search your discard pile for up to 3 Fire Energy cards and attach them to 1 of your Pokémon.

attacks.name: Searing Flame
attacks.effect: The Defending Pokémon is now Burned.

flavorText: Some legends claim that each of its nine tails has its own unique type of special mystical power.

attacks.name: Bulk Up
attacks.effect: During your next turn, each of Lucario's attacks does 30 more damage to the Defending Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: It's said that no foe can remain invisible to Lucario, since it can detect auras, even foes it could not otherwise see.

attacks.name: Confuse Ray
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

attacks.name: Reflect Energy
attacks.effect: Move an Energy card attached to Lanturn to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: The light it emits is so bright that it can illuminate the sea's surface from a depth of over three miles.

attacks.name: Water Arrow
attacks.effect: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. This attack does 30 damage to that Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Stream Pump
attacks.effect: You may do 50 damage plus 30 more damage. If you do, return an Energy card attached to Kingdra to your hand.

flavorText: It sleeps deep on the ocean floor to build its energy. It is said to cause tornadoes as it wakes.
With its flotation sac inflated, it can carry people on its back. It deflates the sac before it dives.

attacks.name: Repeating Drill
attacks.effect: Flip 5 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: It shoots itself suddenly high into the sky, then plummets down in one fell swoop to strike its prey.

attacks.name: Supersonic
attacks.effect: The Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

attacks.name: Hurricane Wing
attacks.effect: Flip 4 coins. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: The development of wings on its legs enables it to fly fast but also makes it tough to stop and rest.

attacks.name: Hydro Launcher
attacks.effect: Return 2 Water Energy attached to Blastoise to your hand. Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. This attack does 100 damage to that Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: It deliberately makes itself heavy so it can withstand the recoil of the water jets it fires.

attacks.name: Twineedle
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 50 damage times the number of heads.

attacks.name: Paralyze Poison
attacks.effect: The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned. Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is also Paralyzed.

flavorText: It can take down any opponent with its powerful poison stingers. It sometimes attacks in swarms.

attacks.name: Psywave
attacks.effect: Does 20 damage times the amount of Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon.

attacks.name: Confuse Ray
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: In South America, it is said that its right eye sees the future and its left eye views the past.

attacks.name: Giga Drain
attacks.effect: Remove from Torterra the number of damage counters equal to the damage you did to the Defending Pokémon.

flavorText: Ancient people imagined that beneath the ground, a gigantic Torterra dwelled.

attacks.name: Push Over
attacks.effect: Does 20 damage times the amount of Fighting Energy attached to Sudowoodo.

attacks.name: Rumble
attacks.effect: The Defending Pokémon can't retreat during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: It disguises itself as a tree to avoid attack. It hates water, so it will disappear if it starts raining.

attacks.name: Energy Bloom
attacks.effect: Remove 3 damage counters from each of your Pokémon that has any Energy attached to it.

flavorText: The blooming of Gracidea flowers confers the power of flight upon it. Feelings of gratitude are the message it delivers.

attacks.name: Big Chorus
attacks.effect: Flip a coin for each Water Pokémon you have in play. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: Whenever three or more of these get together, they sing in a loud voice that sounds like bellowing.

attacks.name: Switch Cannon
attacks.effect: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. This attack does 30 damage to that Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.) Switch Octillery with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Ink Bomb
attacks.effect: If the Defending Pokémon tries to attack during your opponent's next turn, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that attack does nothing.

flavorText: It traps foes with the suction cups on its tentacles, then smashes them with its rock-hard head.

attacks.name: Psychic Pulse
attacks.effect: Does 10 damage to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon that has any damage counters on it. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: Its cries sound like incantations to torment the foe. It appears where you least expect it.

attacks.name: Double Leg Hammer
attacks.effect: Choose 2 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. This attack does 40 damage to each of them. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Deep Sea Swirl
attacks.effect: Shuffle your hand into your deck. Then, draw 5 cards.

flavorText: It starts its life with a wondrous power that permits it to bond with any kind of Pokémon.

attacks.name: Hard Crush
attacks.effect: Discard the top 3 cards from your deck. This attack does 50 damage times the number of Energy cards you discarded.

attacks.name: Mantle Bazooka
attacks.effect: Discard 2 Fire Energy attached to Magmortar.

flavorText: It blasts fireballs of over 3,600 degrees Fahrenheit out of its arms. Its breath also sears and sizzles.

attacks.name: Time Hollow
attacks.effect: Choose a number of your opponent's Stage 1 or Stage 2 Evolved Pokémon up to the amount of Energy attached to Jirachi. Remove the highest Stage Evolution card from each of those Pokémon and put those cards back into your opponent's hand.

flavorText: Generations have believed that any wish written on a note on its head will come true when it awakens.
